protocol.name: ext_input_observer_v1

interface.name: ext_input_observer_v1
interface.description: This interface permits a surface to observe user input, even when the surface does not have the input device's focus.

request.name: pointer_position

description.summary: Creates an observer for the pointer position.

request.description:
Creates a position observer instance for pointer position relative to `surface`.
The seat associated with the surface must have (or had) WL_SEAT_CAPABILITY_POINTER capabilitiey. Otherwise, WL_DISPLAY_ERROR_INVALID_METHOD is raised.

interface.name: ext_input_position_observer_v1
interface.description:
Reports an input device's position relative to the provided surface.
The position is reported also beyond the surface's area, and also when the surface does not have focus. If the surface is mapped more than once, the compositor may report the position relative to each mapped instance of the surface.

event.name: position
event.description:
Reports the input device's position relative to the surface.
The position is reported relative to the surface's edge vectors, and irrespective of whether the surface has input focus or whether the device's position is in- or outside of the surface area.
The relative positions will be reported as signed 16:16 fixpoint values. Positions within the surface will also be reported, with values in the interval [0, 1) for both relative_x and relative_y.
